title: buildah pushes new copies of content it pulled down
body:
Since only a single new layer is produced by the build, the final push should only need to push a single new layer (after checking w/ the registry to confirm the base layers are already present).  Instead multiple layers appear to be getting copied/pushed.  This means builds are slower than they need to be and also we're duplicating content in the registry.

comments.body: @mtrmac PTAL
Yes, I’m working on that in https://github.com/mtrmac/image/tree/blob-info-caching .
@mtrmac any progress?

> @mtrmac any progress?

@rhatdan https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1193#issuecomment-442318275

It looks like this is now fixed in containers/image:

https://github.com/containers/image/pull/526

Is the next step to vendor this into buildah?
That has already happened in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1218 , so this should work as expected with current `master`.
CLosing as it is in master.

It works, but buildah doesn't yet behave completely like docker for this.

We're using artifactory as docker registry and we have multiple registries on it (with multiple hostnames, registry1.tld, registry2.tld), the current code will only check blobs from the same registry although they're also available on the other. 

with docker doing a 
```
docker pull registry1.tld/image:tag
docker tag registry1.tld/image:tag registry2.tld/image2:tag 
docker push registry2.tld/image2:tag
```
will not push the image to registry2.tld because it's already there (i'm not sure if this is default registry behaviour, but it does so with artifactory and docker).

Doing the same with buildah will cause a repush of the image as it's not checking for the existing blob.

Removing the registry sanity checks and setting always the same `scope.Opaque` in the boltdb cache fixes this for me and now buildah behaves the same as docker.

Is there a reason why this isn't the default action, are there security implications ? (as it are sha256 hashes creating collisions there are not feasible)

My changes are here https://github.com/42wim/buildah/commit/4464fa64013b2c7a4584944bff4c67681bb8aa87 in the vendored github.com/containers/image directory
@mtrmac WDYT?
@42wim Could you open an issue with containers/image or better yet a PR so we can discuss it there?

In which version was this released?

I am using buildah 1.7.1 and still see it repeatedly pushing layers originating from `docker://docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine` to my own private repository at `docker://docker.io/<...>` every single time I run `buildah push`. Each time the registry is the same, the repository is the same, and only the tag differs. I would expect buildah to not push the layers from `docker://docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine` at all, but only those that I added.
@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL

How to reproduce:
```
$ buildah pull docker://docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine
$ buildah push docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine docker://docker.io/devurandom/issue-1048

Copying blob 985f0e8ca9de [======================================] 18.6MiB / 18.6MiB
Copying blob c9782632b293 [======================================] 14.0MiB / 14.0MiB
Copying blob f2974ddaacff [======================================] 4.4MiB / 4.4MiB
Copying config e9daa35cb7 [======================================] 6.2KiB / 6.2KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Successfully pushed //devurandom/issue-1048:latest@sha256:8cf2993a0eb9e5b1b39ca198765bb90ac0a0a80156f173c8e8093f8c047436fd
$ buildah push docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine docker://docker.io/devurandom/issue-1048
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob bcf2f368fe23 [==============================>-------] 4.5MiB / 5.5MiB
Copying blob 344fb4b275b7 [======================================] 3.5KiB / 3.5KiB
Copying blob 714e1dd7c2e4 [=>------------------------------------] 5.0MiB / 95.6MiB
Copying blob 985f0e8ca9de [=========>----------------------------] 5.0MiB / 18.6MiB
Copying blob c9782632b293 [============>-------------------------] 4.8MiB / 14.0MiB
Copying blob f2974ddaacff [======================================] 4.4MiB / 4.4MiB
^C⏎
```
(`docker.io/devurandom/issue-1048` is a public repository)

The same also happens when pushing images derived from `docker.io/library/clojure:openjdk-8-lein-2.9.1-alpine` that only add one additional layer and whose name does not start with `docker.io/`, i.e. they are definitely and unmistakably local.

@devurandom thanks for your report.

Can you re-test with `buildah --debug push …`, please? When trying this, I do see debug entries like
```
DEBU[0000] Skipping blob sha256:714e1dd7c2e4561ac781f38261a4985a3e6e9f5aa84b8b1640d73510d1471e61 (already present): 
```

I’m afraid the _progress bars_ always exist in that version, even if no copy actually happens; 
https://github.com/containers/image/pull/603 will make that clearer in the future.

The `--debug` output should also clarify whether/how the cache is being used, either
```
DEBU[0000] Using blob info cache at /var/lib/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb 
```
or a debug entry saying `using a memory-only cache`.

My problem is not with the progress bars per se, but they show that `buildah push` is actually uploading something. (And uploading 95MB takes an awfully long time with my internet connection.)

```

Please note that I redacted the output of environment variables and am showing you only the last two. If you need the value of specific environment variables, please ask for them. Likewise, I also redacted my username.

> ```
> DEBU[0000] Error creating parent directories for blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb, using a memory-only cache: mkdir /var/lib/containers/cache: permission denied
> ```

You are running in some kind of confined `root` account it seems, preventing the recording and use of the data necessary to avoid re-pushing.

> > ```
> > DEBU[0000] Error creating parent directories for blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb, using a memory-only cache: mkdir /var/lib/containers/cache: permission denied
> > ```
> 
> You are running in some kind of confined `root` account it seems, preventing the recording and use of the data necessary to avoid re-pushing.

I am running `buildah` as a regular user. Is something special necessary to support this? For example when when building `buildah` yourself?

To test the hypothesis that the cause is just the writeability of the `/var/lib/containers` directory, I temporarily made `/var/lib/containers` world-writable, but still `buildah` tries to upload the `714e1dd7c2e4` layer. This is even though it for sure is already in the repository, since I previously pushed the whole image. Additionally, I do not see why `buildah push` could not keep the list of layers present in the remote repository in memory to compare them with the layers present locally. A few hashes to not seem too large to hold in RAM?

On the other hand, I cannot find a relation whatsoever between the layers `buildah push` uploads and the layers `skopeo inspect` shows me for `docker://docker.io/devurandom/issue-1048`. Even the smaller `bcf2f368fe23` layer that was pushed first is not present in the repository:
```
$ skopeo inspect docker://docker.io/devurandom/issue-1048 | rg bcf2f368fe23
<no output, exit code 1>
```

Interesting; 

> > > ```
> > > DEBU[0000] Error creating parent directories for blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb, using a memory-only cache: mkdir /var/lib/containers/cache: permission denied
> > > ```
> > 
> > 
> > You are running in some kind of confined `root` account it seems, preventing the recording and use of the data necessary to avoid re-pushing.
> 
> 
> I am running `buildah` as a regular user. Is something special necessary to support this? For example when when building `buildah` yourself?

I’m sorry, you are right; apparently rootless `buildah` does end up with `Geteuid() == 0` at that pull/push path.

Is it _intended_ that `buildah` runs within a user namespace during the pull/push process, without any container being involved?

If so, that gets us into a ~surprising situation that `Geteuid() == 0`, not a different FS namespace (at least, I assume?), but system-wide paths are not accessible.


> To test the hypothesis that the cause is just the writeability of the `/var/lib/containers` directory, I temporarily made `/var/lib/containers` world-writable, but still `buildah` tries to upload the `714e1dd7c2e4`-layer. This is even though it for sure is already in the repository, since I previously pushed the whole image.

FWIW `buildah` can only avoid redundant pushes on the _second_ attempt; the first one uploads the image _and_ records that it has been uploaded and what the result of compressing the image is, the second one uses the recorded compression results to notice that a re-upload is not necessary. Only one push with the cache involved will always do a full upload.

> Is it intended that buildah runs within a user namespace during the pull/push process, without any container being involved?

No idea. I did not do that on purpose. `buildah` must be doing that by itself.

> FWIW buildah can only avoid redundant pushes on the second attempt; the first one uploads the image and records that it has been uploaded and what the result of compressing the image is, the second one uses the recorded compression results to notice that a re-upload is not necessary. Only one push with the cache involved will always do a full upload.

Is it not possible to download the list of layers present in the remote repository before starting to upload anything? Those do not even have to be stored permanently, if the cache directory is not accessible, but should be small enough to hold in memory.

> > Is it intended that buildah runs within a user namespace during the pull/push process, without any container being involved?
> 
> No idea. I did not do that on purpose. `buildah` must be doing that by itself.

Sure; that was a question put to buildah maintainers, I’m sorry about being unclear.

> > FWIW buildah can only avoid redundant pushes on the second attempt; the first one uploads the image and records that it has been uploaded and what the result of compressing the image is, the second one uses the recorded compression results to notice that a re-upload is not necessary. Only one push with the cache involved will always do a full upload.
> 
> Is it not possible to download the list of layers present in the remote repository before starting to upload anything

- No.
- Even if it were possible, the mapping between uncompressed data on local disk and compressed data in the registry needs to exist for the two to match; and that’s what the “blob info cache” is supposed to be.

Can I make `buildah` compress the local data, so that it can compare it directly with the data in the registry?

Some information on the `/var/lib/containers` directory: `buildah push` creates `/var/lib/containers/cache` with my regular user being the owner. There appears to be no other user ID involved, i.e. not even those that I granted to `buildah` using `/etc/subuid`.

> Can I make `buildah` compress the local data, so that it can compare it directly with the data in the registry?

No.

The cache is supposed to work. Let’s just make it work instead of inventing a completely different implementation.

If you are running as non root then /var/lib/containers/cache should not be used.  We should be using ~/.local/share/containers/cache
Could this be the bug?

I’ve reproduced this; apparently rootless buildah ends up with EUID=0 at the point of the image copy, and that’s why the copy code tries to use the root-owned cache path.

(Sure, buildah could override that path, but it’s not just _this_ path; e.g. the path of `~/.docker/config.json` would also point to root’s path if (R)UID==0. So, this seems like a more general design concern — one that I expect has already been considered, with a known solution / design decision. So, I’m asking about the existing design, not trying to reverse-engineer what it is, at this point.)
@giuseppe @mtrmac We can fix the blob path in the buildah patch above.  I am not sure how we fix the ~/.docker/config.json, if this is read after entering the User Namespace.

Regarding the finding-existing-layers-on-first-push issue, just for comparison: `docker push` will also find layers present in other repositories and e.g. report `714e1dd7c2e4: Mounted from library/clojure` on the first push. On subsequent pushes it will report `714e1dd7c2e4: Layer already exists`. (Same for `bcf2f368fe23`, which I tried to find using `skopeo` previously.)
Docker works exactly the same way AFAIK, it’s only that you’ve had a working “cache” recording the necessary data for some time, but with `buildah` you are starting from scratch.
